description: Generic internal IT security risk review framework. Use for organisations without a specific external compliance requirement. Covers all major M365 security domains with plain-language controls suitable for internal audit committees and board reporting.

IR.ID.1.title: Identity Hygiene — User Account Security
IR.ID.1.evidence: MFA coverage, stale/dormant accounts, guest account age and pending invitations, disabled account cleanup

IR.ID.2.title: Privileged Access Management
IR.ID.2.evidence: Global Admin count, permanent vs. time-limited admin access, PIM usage, service principal privilege exposure

IR.AC.1.title: Access Control Policies
IR.AC.1.evidence: Conditional Access policy coverage, legacy auth blocking, password protection, FIDO2/passwordless adoption

IR.DP.1.title: Data Protection Controls
IR.DP.1.evidence: External mail forwarding rules, DMARC/DKIM enforcement, device encryption status, data-in-transit protection

IR.EP.1.title: Endpoint Security
IR.EP.1.evidence: Device compliance percentage, encryption coverage, Defender for Endpoint onboarding, security baselines

IR.TD.1.title: Threat Detection and Monitoring
IR.TD.1.evidence: Identity Protection risk policies, open security alerts, active incidents, Secure Score, audit log coverage

IR.IR.1.title: Incident Response Readiness
IR.IR.1.evidence: Active incident count and age, unresolved high/critical alerts, automated investigation configuration

IR.CM.1.title: Configuration Management
IR.CM.1.evidence: Security baseline deployment, device configuration profiles, Windows Update rings, patch compliance

IR.LM.1.title: License Management and Cost Control
IR.LM.1.evidence: Unused license count and cost waste, duplicate license assignments, trial subscriptions at risk of expiry

IR.GV.1.title: Governance and Oversight
IR.GV.1.evidence: Mailbox audit configuration, application permission hygiene, attack simulation training recency, security awareness

coverage_calculation: Number of controls with at least one passing check / total mapped controls × 100

scoring.note: Internal risk threshold is set at 70% — organisations below this score should prioritise a formal remediation programme
